exemplar.title:
To what extent did the development of dating apps such as Tinder impact the expectations and
concepts of modern relationships?

detailedExemplarData.title:
To what extent did the development of dating apps such as Tinder impact the expectations and
concepts of modern relationships?

strengths: Thorough linkage to a concrete, relevant real-world example
Explicit mapping to course concepts, context and content
weaknesses: Title could be more narrowly tailored to reference the specific lawsuit
Could deepen stakeholder analysis in context section
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission fully meets the top-band descriptor for Inquiry Focus, with a well-defined question and robust justification for its relevance. Minor stylistic refinements are suggested, but overall the work displays a thorough, coherent focus, meriting the full 3 marks.

strengths: Three sources are clearly summarised with purpose and credibility discussed
Justification for each sourceâs usefulness is explicit
Good balance of personal, economic and institutional perspectives
weaknesses: Comparative synthesis of perspectives could be more fully developed
A more critical stance on each sourceâs limitations would strengthen the discussion
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While there is room for deeper synthesis and critique of biases, the treatment of claims and perspectives is structured, coherent and consistently justified. This level of performance aligns with the highest band, justifying the full 6 marks.

strengths: Analysis ties evidence back to the Change concept
Well-supported examples of societal and relational impacts
Sustained, coherent evaluation of multiple stakeholder groups
weaknesses: Could more explicitly connect each impact to quantitative data or case studies
A more systematic breakdown of short- vs long-term implications would enhance depth
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student presents sustained, well-evidenced evaluation of the implications of dating apps on communities, demonstrating effective analysis throughout. This meets the top-band descriptor for Analysis and Evaluation, warranting the full 6 marks.

strengths: Recognises some emerging trends in online dating dynamics
Attempts to draw broader insights back to the inquiry focus
weaknesses: Conclusion lacks depth and fails to discuss future developments in any detail
Insights are relatively cursory, without a clear synthesis of the investigationâs findings
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although a concluding section is present, it provides only limited further insight and addresses future developments in a superficial way. This performance sits in the mid-band for Conclusion, justifying 3 out of 6 marks.

strengths: Logical document structure with clear headings and subheadings
weaknesses: Typographical errors and broken URLs detract from readability
Inconsistent formatting of media titles and inconsistent citation style
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work is organised and legible overall, but formatting inconsistencies and minor errors interrupt the flow. The level of communication corresponds to the upper mid-band descriptor, meriting 3 out of 3 marks.

strandSelections.reasoning: Beyond the source commentary, no sustained analysis of impacts or implications is provided.  There is no evidence of structured evaluation that links empirical evidence to stakeholders or to the concept of Change.  Consequently, what is present is limited and largely descriptive, matching the lower end of band 1-2.

The inquiry process document is logically structured with headings and sub-headings, and citations are generally clear, demonstrating adequate organisation.  However, there are noticeable typographical errors, occasional broken links, and some formatting issues that detract from clarity.  These features align with âadequately organisedâ¦ but not sustained or only partially effectiveâ in band 2.

The student clearly states an inquiry question centred on the effects of dating apps on modern relationships and explicitly links this to a real-world lawsuit against Match Group.  A concise but explicit rationale is provided for why the example is relevant, and the student maps the inquiry to the course concept of Change, the social context of Families and relationships, and the digital-media content area.  Each element required by the rubric (question, real-world example, course concept, content and context) is present and convincingly explained; therefore the work meets the descriptor for a âthorough explanationâ at the top band.

Three sources are examined.  For each, the student summarises the central claim(s), identifies the perspective, and justifies usefulness through purpose, credibility, and scope.  While the discussion is generally thorough, it leans heavily on reliability and usefulness and less on contrasting or synthesising viewpoints; consequently, it fulfils the descriptor for a âpartial discussionâ¦ but not fully developedâ at the upper end of the 3-4 band rather than the consistently sophisticated treatment required for 5-6.

The submission excerpt contains no conclusion section; there is therefore no further insight, discussion of emerging trends, or future developments.  The work does not reach any of the described standards for this strand.

annotations.comment: The inquiry question is clearly stated and well-linked to the real-world example and concepts, demonstrating a focused and thorough Inquiry Focus.
The tools section on page 5 repeats earlier details, which disrupts flow; streamline or combine these entries for clarity.
List of multimedia tools is disconnected from the main analysis; explain how each tool supported your research process.
The real-world example is described, but you could strengthen the link by specifying how the âaddictive designâ claim directly shifts relationship expectations.
The context section outlines relevance to families and relationships, but could deepen the connection by identifying specific stakeholder groups and their changing dynamics.
Strong use of the Change concept definition provides a solid theoretical anchor for analyzing transformation in relationships.
The summary of Source 1 is largely descriptive; consider contrasting its perspective with others to develop deeper synthesis and critique.
The reliability discussion highlights The Guardianâs credibility but omits possible editorial biases; address these limitations to enrich your analysis.
Clear justification of Source 1âs usefulness with examples of both personal narrative and economic context strengthens the inquiry.
Source 2 is introduced without accompanying analysis; ensure each source includes claims, perspectives, and justification for consistency.
The concept section at the start outlines Change well but lacks explicit evaluation of implications; add structured analysis of impacts on stakeholders.
Source 3 description is incomplete and ends abruptly; expand the summary to fully articulate its claims and relevance.
After introducing Source 3, include author credentials and potential perspective to clearly justify its use.
Reliability discussion for the World Economic Forum article could note organizational agendas or funding influences to show critical evaluation.
Analysis of societal impacts is limited; develop a dedicated section linking evidence to specific community and stakeholder implications.
A conclusion section is missing; include a conclusion that synthesizes findings and discusses emerging trends to meet the rubricâs requirements.
URL formatting contains typos (e.g. âthequardianâ); revise all links for accuracy and consistency to improve readability.
Inconsistent italicization of media titles reduces clarity; apply a uniform style for article and video titles throughout.
The multimedia reference list is dense and varied; consider splitting into categories (e.g. figures, videos) to enhance organization.
The tools/applications section is informative but interrupts the inquiry focus; consider relocating it to an appendix.
Icon and image credits lack citation style consistency; align all multimedia citations with your chosen format.
The investigation title is quite broad; refine it to explicitly reference the lawsuit to anchor the inquiry to a specific case.
High word count in Criterion B may impact readability; consider summarizing key points or using bullet lists to focus analysis.
Web source citation omits access date and uses inconsistent styling; ensure all online references include access details and uniform format.
Consider grouping sources thematically rather than simply numbering them to highlight contrasting perspectives more effectively.

strands.name: Inquiry Focus
strands.content: The work does not reach a standard described by the descriptors below.

strands.name: Inquiry Focus
strands.content:
The focus does not include all required elements and/or the real-world example is not
specific or relevant to the inquiry.

strands.name: Inquiry Focus
strands.content: The focus includes an inquiry question and a partial explanation of its connection to a specific, relevant real-world example and course concepts, content and contexts

strands.name: Inquiry Focus
strands.content: The focus includes an inquiry question and a thorough explanation of its connection to a specific, relevant real-world example and course concepts, content and contexts.

strands.name: Claims and perspectives
strands.content: 0 The work does not reach a standard described by the descriptors below.

strands.name: Claims and perspectives
strands.content: The discussion of claims and perspectives is limited and primarily descriptive in nature. Fewer than three sources are discussed or there is no justification for their use in the inquiry.

strands.name: Claims and perspectives
strands.content: There is a partial discussion of the claims and perspectives for each source that includes some justification for their usefulness in the inquiry, but this is not fully developed.

strands.name: Claims and perspectives
strands.content: There is a thorough discussion of the claims and perspectives for each source that includes a clear justification for their usefulness in the inquiry.

strands.name: Analysis and evaluation
strands.content: The work does not reach a standard described by the descriptors below.

strands.name: Analysis and evaluation
strands.content:
There is limited analysis and evaluation which is primarily descriptive in nature or of
limited relevance to the inquiry focus

strands.name: Analysis and evaluation
strands.content: The studentâs analysis and evaluation of impacts and implications for people and communities is adequate, but this is not always sustained or well-supported.

strands.name: Analysis and evaluation
strands.content: The studentâs analysis and evaluation of impacts and implications for people and communities is effective, sustained and well-supported by evidence

strands.name: Conclusion
strands.content: The work does not reach a standard described by the descriptors below.

strands.name: Conclusion
strands.content: The conclusion is limited with little further insight into the inquiry focus. Emerging trends and future developments are referenced with limited or no discussion

strands.name: Conclusion
strands.content: The conclusion provides adequate further insight into the inquiry focus with a partial discussion of emerging trends and future developments

strands.name: Conclusion
strands.content: on

strands.name: Communication
strands.content: The work does not reach a standard described by the descriptors below

strands.name: Communication
strands.content: The presentationâs organization and use of media are limited and do not support understanding.

strands.name: Communication
strands.content: The presentation is adequately organized and the use of media is at times coherent but this is not sustained or only partially effective in supporting understanding.

strands.name: Communication
strands.content: c
